title: distephanus subluteus
intro: Distephanus subluteus, also known as the yellow knapweed or yellow dyssodia, is a flowering plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) native to Mexico and parts of the southwestern United States. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Distephanus subluteus include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Distephanus subluteus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
